mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals tighter labor markets and rising wage pressures, which boost corporate profit margins and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant correlation with UNRATE changes (Pearson r < 0.15) across three consecutive 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY) triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% in more than 40% of cases despite elevated CPI readings.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side expansion, which drives corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with expanding GDP (GDPC1) indicate robust demand-side momentum, which drives equity market optimism and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of HOUST and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns with a 60-day horizon IC below 0.05 in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) creates a 'stagflation' environment, which raises equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 1% in more than 30% of cases despite the dual constraint on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals an overheating labor market, which accelerates wage inflation and prompts monetary policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant negative correlation with UNRATE declines (Pearson r > -0.10) in 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 5-day predictive power for SPX returns falls below 0.02 in 6-month rolling IC tests.
